blocks.content: Cassandra woke in the middle of the night with her phone still beside her and Winston half buried under the blanket.
For a second, she didn't know what had pulled her from sleep.
Then she felt it.
A tightness.
A familiar wrongness moving through her body like a quiet alarm.
She closed her eyes and breathed carefully, counting the way she had been taught.
In for four.
Hold.
Out for six.
Again.
Again.

The room was dark except for the thin line of moonlight across the floor. Somewhere down the hall, the house creaked. Her parents were asleep. Maya was probably not, because Maya slept lightly now, always listening for something she pretended not to fear.
Cassandra reached for the small bottle on her nightstand, then stopped.
For a moment, she just looked at it.
Such a small thing to carry so much meaning.
She took what she needed, swallowed, and leaned back against the pillows.
Her breathing steadied eventually.
But sleep did not return right away.
Instead, she thought about the park.
The sad fountain.
The awkward painter.
The way Ethan had looked at his canvas like he was trying to tell the truth without anyone catching him.
She wondered what he had painted after she left.
She wondered if he had made the fountain less sad.
She wondered if she would see him again.
That last thought was dangerous.
She knew it immediately.
Hope was a beautiful thing until it asked for tomorrow.
Cassandra turned onto her side and held Winston against her chest, smiling faintly into the dark despite herself.
Just a story.
But her heart, stubborn and foolish and very much alive, whispered back:
Maybe the beginning of one.
